fire-alarm-panel.intro: A well-specified fire alarm panel is the control heart of any premises detection system, linking detectors, call points and sounders into one managed interface. iComply Property Services supplies, installs and commissions panels for commercial and multi-occupancy sites across the North West.
fire-alarm-panel.body: Landlords, facilities managers and duty holders rely on a correctly configured panel to meet fire risk assessment actions and insurer expectations. We programme zones, cause-and-effect logic and user access levels, then leave you with clear handover notes and certification. Arrange a site survey online or by phone and we will recommend panel capacity against your building layout.
fire-alarm-panel.meta_desc: Fire alarm panel supply, install and commissioning for commercial sites across the North West. Book iComply today.
focus_points: Panel selection matched to building size and risk
Ongoing service and fault attendance options
faq: How do I know which fire alarm panel size I need?
Panel capacity depends on detector and device counts, zoning strategy and whether you need networked or standalone control. A short site survey confirms the right specification.
Can you replace an existing panel without full system replacement?
Often yes, if field devices remain compatible. We assess loop or radial wiring, device protocols and battery requirements before quoting a panel swap.
What paperwork do I receive after installation?
You receive commissioning records, zone charts, user instructions and any certificates required for compliance evidence and insurer files.
fire-alarm-panel.seo_keywords: fire alarm panel, fire alarm control panel, fire panel installation, fire alarm panel commission, commercial fire panel North West

kentec-fire-alarm-panel.intro: Kentec fire alarm panels are widely specified for addressable and conventional systems where clear engineering interfaces and reliable cause-and-effect matter. iComply installs and services Kentec equipment for Stockport landlords and commercial clients who need manufacturer-aligned setup.
kentec-fire-alarm-panel.body: From compact conventional boards to larger addressable Kentec ranges, we handle mounting, mains and battery supply, loop programming and end-user training. Documentation covers device lists, zone plans and test results so your fire logbook stays audit-ready. Contact us with your existing system make if you are upgrading or expanding a Kentec installation.
kentec-fire-alarm-panel.meta_desc: Kentec fire alarm panel installation and programming in the North West. Spec, commission and certify with iComply.
focus_points: Battery and power supply checks on commission
User training for silence, reset and weekly tests
faq: Are you familiar with specific Kentec models?
Yes. We work with commonly specified Kentec panels and will confirm model suitability against your detection design before ordering hardware.
Can Kentec panels integrate with third-party devices?
Compatibility depends on protocol and interface modules. We verify device lists during design so detectors, sounders and interfaces sit correctly on the system.
Do you offer aftercare for Kentec systems?
We provide planned maintenance visits, fault call-outs and configuration changes when building use or layout evolves.
kentec-fire-alarm-panel.seo_keywords: Kentec fire alarm panel, Kentec fire panel install, Kentec addressable panel, Kentec conventional panel, Kentec fire system North West

eicr.intro: An EICR (Electrical Installation Condition Report) is the formal inspection and testing process that grades the condition of fixed wiring against BS 7671. iComply delivers thorough EICRs for rental stock, workplaces and managed portfolios so you hold current evidence of electrical safety.
eicr.body: Qualified inspectors open distribution boards, sample circuits and record observations with clear C1, C2, C3 or FI coding. You receive a structured report, schedule of circuits and practical remedial advice rather than vague pass-fail language. Book a slot around tenant access windows and we coordinate attendance to minimise disruption.
eicr.meta_desc: EICR testing and Electrical Installation Condition Reports across Manchester and the North West. Clear coding and reports.
focus_points: C1–C3 and FI observations explained in plain English
Circuit schedules and board labelling updates where needed
Remedial quotes aligned to report findings
faq: How often should an EICR be completed?
Private rented homes typically need a report at least every five years or on change of tenancy where required. Commercial intervals depend on risk, use and previous report recommendations.
What if the EICR is unsatisfactory?
Dangerous or potentially dangerous codes need prompt remedial work. We can price and complete repairs, then retest affected circuits so your paperwork reflects a safe installation.
Will tenants need to be present?
Access to all areas with electrical equipment is required. We schedule around occupancy and provide clear arrival windows to reduce missed appointments.
eicr.seo_keywords: EICR, Electrical Installation Condition Report, EICR certificate, EICR testing landlords, EICR North West Manchester

cctv-installation.intro: Professional CCTV installation gives property managers and business owners usable footage, not just cameras on a wall. iComply designs and installs IP and hybrid systems with coverage plans tailored to entrances, yards, corridors and high-value stock areas.
cctv-installation.body: We balance lens choice, night performance and storage retention against your budget and data protection duties. Remote viewing, signage advice and handover training form part of every project so staff know how to export clips when needed. Request a free survey and we will map camera positions before any cabling starts.
cctv-installation.meta_desc: CCTV installation for homes and businesses in the North West. Design, install, remote viewing and clear handover.
focus_points: Site survey and camera placement plans
IP and hybrid recorder options with retention targets
Secure remote access setup for authorised users
faq: How many cameras do I need?
Numbers follow your risk points and viewing goals rather than a flat package size. A survey identifies choke points, blind spots and lighting that affect camera count.
Can footage be viewed on a phone?
Yes. We configure secure apps or browser access for nominated users and explain password hygiene so accounts stay under your control.
Do you install outdoor cameras for car parks?
We routinely fit weather-rated cameras for car parks, loading bays and perimeter fencing, with suitable mounts and cable protection.
cctv-installation.seo_keywords: CCTV installation, CCTV installers, IP CCTV system, security camera installation, commercial CCTV North West

paxton-access.intro: Paxton access control keeps doors, gates and lift lobbies under named credentials instead of shared keys. iComply specifies and installs Paxton Net2 and related hardware for offices, blocks of flats and mixed-use buildings that need auditable entry.
paxton-access.body: Readers, controllers, locks and software are commissioned as one system, with token or fob enrolment and time schedules for cleaners, contractors and out-of-hours staff. Event logs support investigations and tenant queries without guesswork. Speak to us about migrating from standalone digilocks or old magstripe setups to a managed Paxton estate.
paxton-access.meta_desc: Paxton access control installation and programming across the North West. Net2 doors, fobs and audit trails.
focus_points: Paxton Net2 door and multi-door designs
Fob, card and mobile credential options
Time zones, anti-passback and holiday calendars
Integration with fire override and reception workflows
faq: Is Paxton suitable for residential blocks?
Yes. Communal entrance doors, bin stores and plant rooms are common applications, with management software controlled by the freeholder or managing agent.
Can lost fobs be disabled immediately?
Tokens can be barred in software so a lost fob no longer opens doors, without re-keying the entire building.
Do doors unlock when the fire alarm activates?
Where required by the fire strategy, we wire fail-safe release or interface modules so escape routes remain available during an alarm.
paxton-access.seo_keywords: Paxton access control, Paxton Net2 install, Paxton door entry, access control Stockport, Paxton fobs North West

electrical-installation.intro: Electrical installation work covers new circuits, distribution boards, containment and final connections carried out to BS 7671. iComply completes domestic and commercial electrical installations with testing, certification and tidy finishes suitable for occupied buildings.
electrical-installation.body: Projects range from single radial circuits for plant to full floor rewires planned around trading hours. We coordinate with other trades, label boards clearly and issue the correct certificates for Building Control or landlord files. Share drawings or a photo set of your existing boards and we will scope materials and programme.
electrical-installation.meta_desc: Electrical installation to BS 7671 in Manchester and the North West. Circuits, boards, testing and certificates.
focus_points: Design and install of new and extended circuits
Consumer unit and distribution board works
Inspection, testing and Electrical Installation Certificates
faq: Will you notify Building Control if required?
Notifiable work is handled through the appropriate route for the job type. We advise early which elements fall under Part P or commercial notification duties.
Can installation work happen outside normal hours?
Evening and weekend programmes are available for premises that cannot shut down midweek. Pricing reflects access and isolation constraints.
Do you supply materials or use client-supplied stock?
Either model works. We prefer specified brands for protective devices and cable so warranties and compliance remain clear.
electrical-installation.seo_keywords: electrical installation, electrical installers, new circuits installation, distribution board install, electrical installation North West

electrical-maintenance.intro: Planned electrical maintenance stops small defects becoming outages, fire risks or failed inspections. iComply offers scheduled electrical maintenance for multi-site landlords and single commercial premises that need predictable upkeep rather than reactive call-outs alone.
electrical-maintenance.body: Visits can include thermal checks on boards, torque and cleanliness of connections, emergency lighting function tests and logbook updates. Findings feed a prioritised action list so budgets target genuine risk. Set a maintenance calendar with us and we will remind you before each due date.
electrical-maintenance.meta_desc: Electrical maintenance contracts and planned visits across the North West. Fewer faults, clearer compliance logs.
focus_points: Scheduled board and plant electrical checks
Coordination with EICR and fire system service dates
Fixed visit windows to suit occupied sites
faq: What is included in a maintenance visit?
Scope is agreed in the contract and can range from visual and functional checks to measured tests on critical circuits. You receive a visit report after each attendance.
Is maintenance different from an EICR?
Yes. Maintenance keeps systems healthy between formal condition reports. An EICR is a deeper periodic inspection with coded observations and legal weight for many tenancies.
Can you maintain equipment you did not install?
We maintain third-party installations after an initial familiarisation visit to understand boards, drawings and known issues.
electrical-maintenance.seo_keywords: electrical maintenance, planned electrical maintenance, electrical PPM, commercial electrical maintenance, electrical maintenance North West

electrical-services.intro: From testing and repairs to new installs and compliance paperwork, electrical services under one contractor reduce handovers and conflicting advice. iComply provides broad electrical services for property portfolios that need a single accountable team.
electrical-services.body: Whether you manage retail units, offices or residential lets, we align workmanship with BS 7671 and keep certificates in a form your auditors understand. Call-outs, planned jobs and project work can sit on one account with consistent engineer standards. Outline your portfolio size and we will suggest a practical service mix.
electrical-services.meta_desc: Full electrical services for landlords and businesses in the North West — install, test, repair and certify.
focus_points: Install, maintain, inspect and repair under one roof
Responsive engineers familiar with occupied sites
faq: Do you only work on commercial property?
We cover commercial, industrial and residential landlords. Scope and access rules change with property type, which we confirm at booking.
How quickly can you attend?
Routine works are diarised; urgent faults are prioritised where capacity allows. Emergency cover can be discussed for critical sites.
Can invoices be split by property?
Yes. Multi-site clients often receive property-coded invoices to simplify service charge and accounts allocation.
electrical-services.seo_keywords: electrical services, commercial electrical services, landlord electrical services, electrical contractors Stockport, electrical services North West

certified-electrician.intro: Hiring a certified electrician protects your property, tenants and insurance position when fixed wiring or notifiable work is involved. iComply deploys certified electricians who test as they work and leave the right certificates rather than informal notes.
certified-electrician.body: Competence shows in safe isolation, correct device selection and honest reporting when an installation needs more than a quick fix. Homeowners and duty holders can check scheme status and receive Installation or Minor Works Certificates as appropriate. Describe the symptom or project brief and we assign a suitable certified electrician.
certified-electrician.meta_desc: Certified electrician for homes and businesses across Manchester and the North West. Tested, certified work.
focus_points: Safe isolation and methodical fault diagnosis
Advice when upgrades beat repeated repairs
faq: What does certified mean in practice?
It means the electrician is assessed under a recognised competence scheme and works to current Wiring Regulations, issuing statutory-style paperwork where required.
Will I always get a certificate?
New circuits, rewires and many alterations need formal certificates. Purely diagnostic visits may produce a report instead if no installation work is carried out.
Can a certified electrician sign off someone else’s work?
Signing off requires adequate inspection and testing of that work. We only certify installations we have properly verified.
certified-electrician.seo_keywords: certified electrician, qualified electrician, registered electrician, competent electrician, certified electrician North West

electrical-contractor.intro: An electrical contractor coordinates labour, materials and testing so multi-trade projects stay on programme. iComply acts as electrical contractor for refurbishments, fit-outs and compliance programmes for clients who need more than a one-job tradesperson.
electrical-contractor.body: We price from drawings or measured surveys, manage isolation permits on live sites and deliver progressive testing rather than a rush at practical completion. Site managers get one point of contact for variations and as-fitted information. Send tender documents or a scope list for a contractor-grade response.
electrical-contractor.meta_desc: Electrical contractor for fit-outs and refurbishments in the North West. Programmed works, testing and certs.

Can you resource larger multi-week jobs?
Team size scales with programme. We flag long-lead items early so boards, containment and specialty fittings do not stall the build.
How are variations handled?
Extra works are quoted or day-worked by agreement before they proceed, with written confirmation for your cost control.

electrical-repair.intro: Electrical repair restores safe function when sockets fail, boards trip or lighting circuits go dark without a full rewire. iComply diagnoses and completes electrical repairs with parts that match the installation’s protective scheme.
electrical-repair.body: Guesswork swaps of breakers without finding the cause are avoided; we measure, isolate and prove the fix. Where age or damage makes repair uneconomic, you get a straight recommendation to replace the circuit or board section. Call with photos of the board and a description of when the fault occurs.
electrical-repair.meta_desc: Electrical repair for trips, dead circuits and damaged fittings across the North West. Diagnose, fix, certify.
focus_points: Socket, switch and light circuit repairs
Distribution board device replacement where suitable
Advice when repair should become upgrade
faq: Why does my breaker keep tripping after a previous repair?
Residual faults, overloaded circuits or incorrect breaker ratings can leave intermittent trips. A structured fault find identifies the true cause.
Do you repair outdoor and garden electrical issues?
Yes, including exterior lighting and supplies to outbuildings, subject to safe isolation and weatherproofing standards.
Is repair work certified?
Like-for-like replacements and minor works often receive a Minor Electrical Installation Works Certificate. Larger alterations use full installation certification.
electrical-repair.seo_keywords: electrical repair, electrician repairs, circuit repair, fuse board repair, electrical repair North West

Do you handle landlord and tenant split supplies?

residential-electrician.intro: A residential electrician focuses on homes, HMOs and small blocks where comfort, safety and neat finishing matter as much as compliance. iComply’s residential electricians handle consumer units, lighting schemes, EV readiness and landlord safety work with care in lived-in spaces.

electrical-wiring-installation.intro: Electrical wiring installation is the backbone that carries power safely from the intake to every outlet and luminaire. iComply installs wiring systems using correct cable types, containment and segregation for data where required.
electrical-wiring-installation.body: Whether chasing walls in a refurbishment or running surface trunking in a commercial back-of-house area, routes are planned for future maintenance access. Continuity, insulation resistance and polarity tests prove each circuit before energising. Provide floor plans if you have them and we will mark proposed routes for approval.
electrical-wiring-installation.meta_desc: Electrical wiring installation to BS 7671 across the North West. Cable routes, testing and full certification.
focus_points: Correct cable selection for load and environment
Segregation guidance near data and fire cables
faq: Is rewiring always needed in older properties?
Not always. Condition, earthing, bonding and protective devices decide whether a full rewire, partial rewire or targeted upgrades are proportionate.
Do you install steel wire armour for external runs?
SWA and other external methods are used where mechanical protection or underground routes demand them.
How disruptive is new wiring in a finished home?
Some making-good is inevitable with chased cables. We discuss surface options where you want to avoid extensive plaster repairs.
electrical-wiring-installation.seo_keywords: electrical wiring installation, house rewiring, cable installation electrician, new wiring circuits, wiring installation North West

new-electrical-installation.intro: A new electrical installation starts from intake arrangements, earthing and distribution design rather than bolting circuits onto an unknown board. iComply delivers new electrical installations for extensions, new builds and stripped refurbishments that need a clean compliance baseline.
new-electrical-installation.body: Load assessment, board schedules and first-fix principles guide cable sizes and protective devices. Final inspection and testing produce an Electrical Installation Certificate with schedules you can file for warranty and Building Control. Engage us at first fix stage so back boxes and conduit land in the right places.
new-electrical-installation.meta_desc: New electrical installation for builds and extensions in Manchester and the North West. Design, install, certify.
focus_points: Earthing, bonding and surge protection options
faq: When should the electrician attend a new build?
Ideally before first fix closes up, then again for second fix and final testing. Early involvement avoids costly chasing later.
Can you work to an architect’s electrical layout?
Yes. We review layouts for regulation compliance and practicality, flagging clashes with furniture, kitchens or HVAC.
Who arranges the DNO connection?
Service head and meter works often involve the District Network Operator or an independent connection provider. We coordinate consumer-side works around their programme.
new-electrical-installation.seo_keywords: new electrical installation, new build electrician, extension electrical install, Electrical Installation Certificate, new installation North West

lighting-installation.intro: Lighting installation shapes how safe and usable a space feels after dark, from emergency routes to retail displays. iComply designs and installs interior and exterior lighting with efficient LED sources and compliant controls.
lighting-installation.body: We consider lux levels, glare, switching zones and emergency coverage rather than simply swapping lamps. lighting-installation.meta_desc: Lighting installation for commercial and residential sites in the North West. LED, emergency and external lighting.
focus_points: LED upgrades and new lighting schemes
Emergency lighting install and spacing advice
Controls for energy saving and user comfort
faq: Can you reuse existing wiring for new lights?
Often yes if the circuit condition and earthing are sound. We test before relying on old cables for a new lighting load.
Do you install emergency lighting exit routes?
We install maintained and non-maintained emergency luminaires and can integrate testing regimes into your maintenance plan.
Will LED conversion always reduce my bill?
LEDs typically cut wattage significantly, especially when paired with sensible controls. Exact savings depend on hours of use and existing lamp types.
lighting-installation.seo_keywords: lighting installation, LED lighting install, emergency lighting installation, outdoor lighting electrician, lighting installation North West

ev-charger-installation.intro: EV charger installation needs more than a wall box: supply capacity, cable routes, isolation and notification rules all matter. iComply installs domestic and workplace EV chargers with load assessment so your main fuse and board remain within safe limits.
ev-charger-installation.body: We survey intake tails, earthing arrangements and parking positions, then recommend tethered or socket units with smart charging where useful. OZEV grant eligibility and DNO notification are handled according to the installation type. Book a survey with photos of your consumer unit and parking bay to speed the quote.
ev-charger-installation.meta_desc: EV charger installation across the North West. Load checks, smart chargers, safe install and certification.
focus_points: Smart scheduling and app setup support
faq: Is my fuseboard big enough for an EV charger?
Not every home or workplace has spare capacity. A survey measures available headroom and may recommend a supply upgrade or load management.
How long does a standard home install take?
Many straightforward installs complete in a day once hardware is on site. Complex cable runs or board upgrades add time.
Can multiple bays share load management?
Workplace sites often use load-balanced multi-outlet systems so several vehicles charge without overloading the intake.
ev-charger-installation.seo_keywords: EV charger installation, electric car charger install, home EV charger, workplace EV charging, EV charger North West

generator-installation.intro: Generator installation provides standby or prime power when grid failure would halt operations or compromise safety systems. iComply plans generator electrical interfaces, changeover arrangements and protection so transfer to generator power is controlled and testable.
generator-installation.body: Fuel, exhaust, acoustic and structural elements may involve specialist partners; our focus is electrical integration, earthing, interlocks and commissioning tests. Weekly or monthly test schedules can be written into your maintenance plan. Share load lists and critical circuit priorities so sizing and transfer logic match real need.
generator-installation.meta_desc: Generator installation and electrical integration in the North West. Changeover, protection and commissioning.
faq: Do you supply the generator set as well as electrics?
We can coordinate package supply or work with a generator you already own. Electrical scope is always defined clearly in the quote.
Will the generator power the whole building?
Some sites support full load; others only life-safety and critical IT. Load prioritisation is agreed before installation.
How often should generators be tested?
Manufacturers and insurers often expect regular no-load or load bank tests. We help set a realistic test routine for your risk profile.
generator-installation.seo_keywords: generator installation, standby generator install, backup power electrician, ATS changeover install, generator installation North West

solar-panel-installation.intro: Solar panel installation turns roof space into on-site generation when structural, electrical and grid connection factors align. iComply supports solar PV electrical works including AC connection, isolation, generation meters and compliance with relevant standards.
solar-panel-installation.body: Roof mounting and panel supply may be packaged with partners; electrical safety, G98/G99 considerations and consumer unit integration remain central to a durable system. Monitoring setup helps you verify output after handover. Request a desk study with roof photos and a recent electricity bill to start feasibility.
solar-panel-installation.meta_desc: Solar panel installation support and PV electrical connection across the North West. Safe grid-tied systems.
faq: Can solar work on a shared or flat roof?
Many roof types are viable if structure, waterproofing and orientation allow. Specialist roofing input is included where needed.
What happens during a power cut?
Standard grid-tied systems shut down for safety unless battery and islanding features are designed in. We explain modes clearly before you order.
Do I need a new consumer unit for solar?
Not always, but spare ways, surge protection and correct isolation are mandatory checks. Board upgrades are quoted only when justified.
solar-panel-installation.seo_keywords: solar panel installation, solar PV install, rooftop solar electrician, grid tied solar, solar panel installation North West

panel-upgrade.intro: A panel upgrade—replacing or expanding a consumer unit or distribution board—improves protection, capacity and clarity of circuit labelling. iComply carries out panel upgrades when breakers are obsolete, RCDs are missing or additional ways are needed for modern loads.
panel-upgrade.body: We photograph and map existing circuits, plan changeover downtime and test every outgoing circuit before sign-off. Metal boards, dual RCD or all-RCBO layouts are chosen against risk and budget. Send a clear photo of your current board interior to begin scoping.
panel-upgrade.meta_desc: Consumer unit and distribution panel upgrade in the North West. Safer protection, more ways, full testing.
focus_points: Consumer unit and three-phase board upgrades
faq: How long will power be off during a panel upgrade?
Domestic upgrades often need several hours of isolation. Commercial boards may be phased by section if the installation allows.
Must all circuits be rewired for a new board?
No. A panel upgrade re-terminates existing circuits if they are sound. Defective circuits found during testing are quoted separately.
Are old rewirable fuse boards still acceptable?
They rarely meet modern expectations for additional protection. Upgrading is strongly recommended for safety and insurance comfort.
panel-upgrade.seo_keywords: panel upgrade, consumer unit upgrade, fuse board replacement, distribution board upgrade, panel upgrade North West

smart-home-wiring.intro: Smart home wiring lays structured power and data paths so lighting, shading, audio and security devices work reliably rather than relying on weak wireless links alone. iComply installs smart home wiring during refurbishments and new builds when walls are open and future-proofing is cheapest.
smart-home-wiring.body: Backbone cabling, dedicated supplies for hubs and thoughtful switch drop positions prevent the clutter of later retrofits. We coordinate with your chosen automation brand where protocols need specific cable types. Share your device wish-list early so first fix captures enough drops.
smart-home-wiring.meta_desc: Smart home wiring and structured cabling in the North West. Future-ready power and data for automation.
faq: Is wireless smart gear enough without extra wiring?
Wireless suits many retrofits, but wired backbones remain more reliable for dense device counts and whole-home lighting control.
Which cable types do you install?
Typically Cat6 or better for data, plus appropriately rated power circuits. Specialty bus cables follow the automation brand specification.
Can you add smart wiring to a finished house?
Yes, using careful surface routes, loft runs or selective chasing. Impact is higher than first-fix installs but still manageable with planning.
smart-home-wiring.seo_keywords: smart home wiring, home automation wiring, structured wiring home, smart lighting prewire, smart home wiring North West

data-cabling-installation.intro: Data cabling installation underpins stable networks for offices, clinics and residential blocks with shared plant rooms. iComply installs structured data cabling with neat patch panels, tested links and routes that respect fire compartmentation.
data-cabling-installation.body: Cat6 and higher grades are terminated to TIA/ISO practices with certification test results on request. We separate power and data where practicable and label every outlet for easy IT handover. Provide a floor plan with desk and AP locations for an accurate point count.
data-cabling-installation.meta_desc: Data cabling installation for offices and multi-site portfolios in the North West. Cat6, patching and tests.
focus_points: Patch panels, cabinets and tidy looms
Containment shared carefully with power routes
faq: Do you install fibre as well as copper?
Fibre backbones between floors or buildings can be included. We confirm connector types and termination method in the quote.
Can cabling run through occupied offices?
Yes. We use low-dust methods and out-of-hours options for busy workplaces.
Will you move existing network switches?
Cabinet moves and re-patching can form part of the project so IT teams inherit a clean, documented rack.
data-cabling-installation.seo_keywords: data cabling installation, Cat6 installation, structured cabling, network cabling installers, data cabling North West

electrical-inspection.intro: Electrical inspection is the systematic visual and, where required, measured review of fixed installations before problems escalate. iComply performs electrical inspections for due diligence, pre-lease checks and routine assurance between full EICR cycles.
electrical-inspection.body: Inspectors look for damaged accessories, overheating signs, inadequate bonding and board congestion that pure desktop reviews miss. Findings arrive in plain language with photos where useful so decision makers can prioritise spend. Book inspection alone or pair it with testing if you need formal certification.
electrical-inspection.meta_desc: Electrical inspection services across Manchester and the North West. Clear findings for landlords and managers.
focus_points: Suitable for pre-lease and portfolio audits
Pathway to full EICR testing if needed
faq: Is an inspection the same as an EICR?
An EICR includes formal inspection and testing with coded outcomes. A lighter inspection may be visual-only or limited testing by agreement.
How long does an inspection take?
A flat may take a short visit; large commercial floors take longer. Size, access and board count drive the duration.
Can inspections be done without tenant notice?
Access still requires legal and practical permission. We work within your notice process and privacy expectations.
electrical-inspection.seo_keywords: electrical inspection, electrical inspection service, fixed wire inspection, pre lease electrical check, electrical inspection North West

electrical-troubleshooting.intro: Electrical troubleshooting tracks intermittent trips, voltage complaints and mysterious dead spots using methodical testing rather than part-swapping. iComply electricians specialise in electrical troubleshooting for sites where downtime costs more than a thorough diagnosis.
electrical-troubleshooting.body: We recreate fault conditions safely, use instruments suited to the symptom and document likely causes with evidence. Recurring issues sometimes need thermal imaging or longer monitoring; we say when that step is justified. Note when the fault occurs—after rain, under load, at startup—to sharpen the first visit.
electrical-troubleshooting.meta_desc: Electrical troubleshooting for intermittent trips and odd faults in the North West. Diagnose first, then repair.
faq: Why can’t every fault be fixed on the first visit?
Intermittent problems may need monitoring or access to areas unavailable on day one. We explain next steps rather than guessing.
Do you troubleshoot machinery as well as building wiring?
Building electrical systems are our core. Machine OEM faults may need a specialist; we isolate whether the supply side is healthy.
Will troubleshooting include a written report?
Yes when requested or when findings affect compliance. Simple household fixes may be summarised on the job sheet.
electrical-troubleshooting.seo_keywords: electrical troubleshooting, electrical fault diagnosis, intermittent trip electrician, electrical diagnostics, electrical troubleshooting North West

circuit-breaker-repair.intro: Circuit breaker repair and replacement restores protection when devices fail to reset, overheat or no longer match circuit design. iComply assesses whether a breaker can be replaced like-for-like or whether board limitations force a wider panel upgrade.
circuit-breaker-repair.body: Incorrect ratings and mixed manufacturer stacks create hidden risks; we check cable size, load and discrimination before fitting new devices. RCBO conversions are common where individual circuit protection is preferred. Do not keep resetting a breaker that trips immediately—call for diagnosis first.
circuit-breaker-repair.meta_desc: Circuit breaker repair and RCBO replacement across the North West. Safe ratings, testing and board advice.
focus_points: Board compatibility checks before parts order
faq: Is a breaker that feels hot always faulty?
Heat can mean overload, loose terminations or a failing device. Measurement and inspection identify which applies.
Can I upgrade one breaker without changing the whole board?
Often yes if the board accepts the device type. Some older boards have limited or obsolete spares, which we flag early.
What is the difference between MCB and RCBO?
An MCB provides overcurrent protection; an RCBO adds residual current protection on the same device for that circuit.
circuit-breaker-repair.seo_keywords: circuit breaker repair, MCB replacement, RCBO install, breaker trips electrician, circuit breaker North West

electrical-fault-finding.intro: Electrical fault finding isolates short circuits, earth faults and open circuits with instruments and logical sectioning of the installation. iComply offers electrical fault finding for landlords and businesses who need the real cause identified before redecoration or tenant return.
electrical-fault-finding.body: Dead testing and live investigation are chosen according to risk and symptom. Once located, repairs are quoted with options ranked by urgency. Keep a note of recent works, water leaks or DIY changes—these clues often shorten the search.
electrical-fault-finding.meta_desc: Electrical fault finding for shorts, earth faults and open circuits in the North West. Fast, methodical diagnosis.
focus_points: Short circuit and earth fault location
Open circuit and high resistance joint finding
faq: Will fault finding damage decoration?
We minimise opening-up, starting with non-invasive tests. Some hidden faults still need access panels or limited chasing.
Can you find faults under floors or in walls?
Yes, using sectional testing and cable tools. Exact methods depend on construction type and access.
Is fault finding charged if I proceed with the repair?
Diagnostic time is usually itemised; repair labour follows agreement. Packages can be discussed for straightforward jobs.
electrical-fault-finding.seo_keywords: electrical fault finding, find electrical fault, earth fault electrician, short circuit diagnosis, electrical fault finding North West

electrical-safety-certificate.intro: An electrical safety certificate is the formal record that installation or inspection work met the required standard on the date of issue. iComply issues electrical safety certificates appropriate to the job—Installation, Minor Works or condition reports—so agents and insurers receive recognised paperwork.
electrical-safety-certificate.body: Certificates list circuit details, test results and the responsible person, forming part of tenancy and sale packs. We never issue paperwork without performing the underlying inspection or installation verification. Tell us which document your agent or lender requested if you are unsure of the title.
electrical-safety-certificate.meta_desc: Electrical safety certificate issue across the North West. Installation, minor works and condition report docs.
focus_points: Correct certificate type for the work done
Test results recorded against each circuit
Suitable for agents, lenders and insurers
Remedial path if certification cannot yet be given
faq: Which certificate do landlords need?
Private landlords in England typically need a current EICR for the fixed wiring, plus certificates for any new works carried out.
How long is a certificate valid?
Installation certificates record work at a point in time; EICRs recommend a next inspection date. Validity for legal duties depends on the document type and regime.
Can you reissue a lost certificate?
If we completed the original works and records remain, we can often supply a copy. Third-party historic certificates cannot be recreated from scratch.
electrical-safety-certificate.seo_keywords: electrical safety certificate, electrical certificate, installation certificate, electrical compliance certificate, electrical safety certificate North West

niceic-certified.intro: NICEIC certified status signals that an electrical business meets assessed competence and quality standards recognised across the industry. iComply’s NICEIC certified approach gives clients confidence that design, install and test processes are audited rather than informal.
niceic-certified.body: Scheme membership supports Building Control notification routes for many domestic jobs and reassures commercial clients during vendor approval. You still receive full test results and certificates specific to your property. Ask for scheme details at tender stage if your procurement checklist requires them.
niceic-certified.meta_desc: NICEIC certified electrical work across Manchester and the North West. Assessed competence you can verify.
focus_points: Notification pathways for qualifying domestic work
faq: How can I check NICEIC certification?
Scheme websites allow searches by business name. We can also provide membership details on request for your compliance file.
Does NICEIC certified mean a guarantee on all work?
Scheme rules and warranties depend on membership type and job category. Contractual warranties for your project are stated in our paperwork.
Is NICEIC mandatory for every electrical job?
Not every task legally requires scheme membership, but many clients and Building Control routes expect a registered competent person.
niceic-certified.seo_keywords: NICEIC certified, NICEIC electrician, NICEIC registered, NICEIC contractor, NICEIC certified North West

part-p-certified.intro: Part P certified electricians understand the Building Regulations requirements that apply to electrical work in dwellings in England. iComply undertakes Part P relevant works with self-certification routes where scheme membership allows, reducing hassle for homeowners and landlords.
part-p-certified.body: Not all electrical tasks are notifiable, but new circuits, consumer unit changes and work in bathrooms often are. We advise which jobs need notification and complete the process as part of the package. Describe your planned kitchen or bathroom project and we will map Part P duties before work starts.
part-p-certified.meta_desc: Part P certified electrical works for dwellings in the North West. Notifiable jobs handled with correct certs.
focus_points: Consumer unit and new circuit compliance
faq: What is Part P in simple terms?
Part P is the Building Regulations part covering electrical safety in dwellings. It aims to ensure work is designed and installed to avoid danger.
Do like-for-like socket swaps need Part P notification?
Many minor like-for-like replacements are non-notifiable, but condition and earthing still matter. We confirm classification per job.
What if a DIY relative already started the wiring?
We can inspect and test, but we only certify work that meets standards. Rework may be required before certification is possible.
part-p-certified.seo_keywords: Part P certified, Part P electrician, Building Regulations electrical, notifiable electrical work, Part P certified North West

pat-testing.intro: PAT testing (portable appliance testing) checks plugs, leads and portable equipment for faults that fixed-wire EICRs do not cover. iComply delivers PAT testing for offices, landlords of furnished lets and facilities teams who need asset labels and registers.
pat-testing.body: Appliances are classed, tested and labelled with pass or fail outcomes, then listed in a digital-friendly register for your health and safety file. Failed items are isolated from use with clear next steps. Schedule annual or risk-based intervals and we will remind you before labels expire.
pat-testing.meta_desc: PAT testing for offices and landlords across the North West. Labels, registers and failed-item advice.
focus_points: Formal visual and instrument checks on portables
Asset register for audits and insurers
Risk-based retest intervals agreed with you
faq: Is PAT testing a legal annual requirement?
Law requires maintained electrical equipment that is safe; frequency follows risk assessment rather than a single universal date. Many workplaces still test yearly for simplicity.
Do you test microwave leakage or just electrical safety?
Standard PAT focuses on electrical safety of the appliance and flex. Specialist microwave leakage tests can be discussed if needed.
Can staff remain at their desks during testing?
Brief power-downs are needed per item. We work section by section to limit disruption.
pat-testing.seo_keywords: PAT testing, portable appliance testing, PAT test certificate, office PAT testing, PAT testing North West

landlord-electrical-certificate.intro: A landlord electrical certificate usually means a current EICR plus any works certificates needed to prove rental properties meet electrical safety duties. iComply helps landlords obtain landlord electrical certificates with coded reports and remedial completion where required.
landlord-electrical-certificate.body: We understand tenancy turnaround pressure and can target voids or coordinate tenanted access. Reports are written so agents can file them without chasing missing schedules. Register your portfolio for recurring five-year cycles and change-of-tenancy checks.
landlord-electrical-certificate.meta_desc: Landlord electrical certificate and EICR services in the North West. Reports, remedials and retest options.
focus_points: EICRs aligned to private rental duties
Remedial works and satisfactory reissue path
faq: How quickly must remedials be done after an unsatisfactory report?
Dangerous conditions need immediate action. Other codes have recommended timescales; we help you plan against risk and legal expectations.
Do furnished rentals also need PAT?
Portable appliances supplied with the let should be maintained safely. PAT is a practical way to evidence that duty alongside the fixed-wire EICR.
Can one certificate cover a whole HMO?
An EICR covers the installation inspected. HMOs may need careful scope definition for landlord and tenant supplies—we clarify boundaries on site.
landlord-electrical-certificate.seo_keywords: landlord electrical certificate, landlord EICR, rental electrical safety certificate, HMO electrical certificate, landlord electrical certificate North West

periodic-inspection-report.intro: A periodic inspection report—commonly delivered today as an EICR—records the condition of an electrical installation at set intervals. iComply produces periodic inspection reports that facilities managers can action, not just file.
periodic-inspection-report.body: Sampling strategies, limitation statements and observation codes are explained so you know what was and was not covered. Trends across multi-year reports help justify capital upgrades to boards and submains. Align periodic dates with other compliance visits to cut access costs.
periodic-inspection-report.meta_desc: Periodic inspection report (EICR) services across Manchester and the North West. Actionable coded findings.
focus_points: Scheduling tied to portfolio compliance calendars
faq: Why do reports list limitations?
Some areas are unsafe or impossible to access fully. Honest limitations protect you from assuming untested circuits were proven sound.
What interval should commercial sites use?
Risk, environment and previous report advice drive intervals—often three to five years, sometimes less for harsh conditions.
Can you compare this report with our last one?
Yes. Bring prior paperwork and we will highlight recurring issues and completed remedials.
periodic-inspection-report.seo_keywords: periodic inspection report, periodic electrical inspection, EICR periodic, fixed wire periodic test, periodic inspection report North West

bs-7671.intro: BS 7671, the IET Wiring Regulations, is the UK standard that governs design, installation and testing of electrical installations. iComply works to BS 7671 so new works and inspections reflect current Amendment requirements rather than outdated practice.
bs-7671.body: Surge protection, AFDD consideration, RCD provisions and documentation rules evolve with each amendment; our engineers apply the edition relevant to your project. Clients receive certificates that reference the regulation framework assessors expect. Ask us how a recent amendment affects a planned board change or EV circuit.
bs-7671.meta_desc: BS 7671 compliant electrical design, install and testing in the North West. Current Wiring Regulations practice.
focus_points: Installations aligned to current BS 7671 amendments
RCD, SPD and special location requirements
Inspection and testing to regulation methods
Guidance when older installs pre-date new rules
faq: Does BS 7671 apply to commercial buildings?
Yes. It applies broadly to electrical installations in and around buildings, with additional standards sometimes relevant for specialist environments.
Must every existing installation be upgraded to the latest amendment?
Not automatically. New work must meet current requirements; existing installations are judged on safety and condition during inspection.
Where can I read BS 7671 myself?
The standard is published by the IET/BSI. We translate the clauses that affect your project into practical recommendations.
bs-7671.seo_keywords: BS 7671, Wiring Regulations, BS 7671 compliance, 18th Edition electrical, BS 7671 North West

24-hour-emergency-electrician.intro: A 24 hour emergency electrician responds when power loss, burning smells, flooding near electrics or repeated dangerous tripping cannot wait for office hours. iComply offers urgent electrical attendance aimed at making safe first, then planning lasting repairs.
24-hour-emergency-electrician.body: Night and weekend call-outs focus on isolation, temporary supplies where safe, and clear advice if full repair needs daylight or parts. You will know attendance terms before an engineer is despatched. Use the emergency line for genuine safety or business-critical outages rather than routine upgrades.
24-hour-emergency-electrician.meta_desc: 24 hour emergency electrician cover in the North West. Make-safe response for urgent electrical dangers.
focus_points: Board and supply failure first response
faq: What counts as an electrical emergency?
Burning odours, sparking, partial power with safety risk, water in boards or total loss affecting vulnerable occupants typically qualify.
Will the engineer complete full repairs at 2am?
Make-safe is the priority. Permanent repairs proceed when parts, access and safe working conditions allow—sometimes the next day.
Are emergency rates different from daytime rates?
Out-of-hours attendance usually carries a premium. Costs are explained when you call so you can decide before despatch.
24-hour-emergency-electrician.seo_keywords: 24 hour emergency electrician, emergency electrician Stockport, out of hours electrician, urgent electrical call out, emergency electrician North West

electrical-installation-condition-report.intro: An Electrical Installation Condition Report is the full title behind the familiar EICR acronym, detailing the health of fixed wiring after inspection and testing. iComply produces Electrical Installation Condition Reports that satisfy agents, insurers and duty holders who need complete schedules and codes.
electrical-installation-condition-report.body: Every report includes installation details, extent and limitations, observation lists and a summary satisfactory or unsatisfactory outcome. We walk clients through next inspection dates and remedial categories so nothing sits misunderstood in a PDF folder. Book standalone reports or combine with remedial delivery for a single project owner.
electrical-installation-condition-report.meta_desc: Electrical Installation Condition Report (EICR) across the North West. Full schedules, codes and next steps.
faq: Who should receive a copy of the report?
The client commissioning the work—often landlord, freeholder or facilities manager—and sometimes tenants or agents by agreement.
What does FI mean on a report?
Further Investigation is required where the inspector could not fully determine condition. FI items should be closed out promptly.
Can you test only part of a large building?
Extent can be agreed, but limitations must be honest. Partial reports should not be presented as covering untested areas.
electrical-installation-condition-report.seo_keywords: Electrical Installation Condition Report, EICR full name, condition report electrical, fixed installation condition report, Electrical Installation Condition Report North West

industrial-electrical-services.intro: Industrial electrical services cover heavier distribution, machinery supplies, containment in harsh environments and disciplined isolation procedures. iComply supports light industrial units and production sites that need industrial electrical services without sacrificing documentation quality.
industrial-electrical-services.body: From submain alterations to socket drops on production lines, we plan permits, lock-off and reinstatement testing around shift patterns. Dust, vibration and moisture influence equipment choice; we specify accordingly. Invite us for a plant walkaround to capture realistic scope and downtime needs.
industrial-electrical-services.meta_desc: Industrial electrical services for workshops and units in the North West. Submains, plant supplies, safe isolation.
faq: Do you work on high-voltage networks?
Our focus is low-voltage industrial installations. HV works require specialist providers; we can coordinate at the LV interface.
Can you install power for new CNC or compressor plant?
Yes, including cable calculations, isolators and any local distribution needed, subject to intake capacity.
Are night shutdowns possible?
Planned isolations during night or weekend shutdowns are common and can be resourced with notice.
industrial-electrical-services.seo_keywords: industrial electrical services, industrial electrician, factory electrical contractor, workshop electrical install, industrial electrical North West

kitchen-electrical-installation.intro: Kitchen electrical installation must handle high-load appliances, correct zoning near sinks and durable accessories that survive daily use. iComply plans kitchen electrical installation alongside fitters so hob, oven, extraction and island sockets land exactly where units allow.
kitchen-electrical-installation.body: We check diversity on cooker circuits, provide fused connection units where required and avoid DIY dangling flexes behind panels. Extractor fans, under-cabinet lighting and appliance RFCs are coordinated in one visit where possible. Share kitchen plans with appliance specs for a first-fix and second-fix programme.
kitchen-electrical-installation.meta_desc: Kitchen electrical installation in the North West. Cooker circuits, sockets, extractors and safe zoning.
faq: When should the electrician attend a kitchen refit?
First fix before units go in, then second fix after worktops and appliance delivery. Missing first fix causes expensive delays.
Do induction hobs need special supplies?
Many need dedicated circuits and correct cable size. We verify manufacturer instructions before terminating.
Can you add sockets after units are fitted?
Yes, but access is harder. Surface trunking or careful rear access may be needed; planning ahead is cleaner.
kitchen-electrical-installation.seo_keywords: kitchen electrical installation, kitchen electrician, cooker circuit install, kitchen sockets installation, kitchen electrical North West

bathroom-electrical-safety.intro: Bathroom electrical safety hinges on zone rules, IP ratings, RCD protection and sensible equipment choices in wet areas. iComply upgrades and inspects bathrooms so lights, fans and heaters meet current expectations for bathroom electrical safety.
bathroom-electrical-safety.body: We replace unsuitable fittings, correct bonding issues and ensure extraction and lighting controls sit outside prohibited zones where required. Landlords and homeowners both benefit from clear guidance before tiling locks in mistakes. Send photos of existing fittings and the consumer unit for remote advice ahead of a visit.
bathroom-electrical-safety.meta_desc: Bathroom electrical safety upgrades and checks across the North West. Zones, IP ratings and RCD protection.
focus_points: Safe selector switches and pull cords
faq: Are power showers and electric towel rails allowed?
Yes when installed to zone and manufacturer rules with correct protection. We confirm product suitability before fitting.
Can I have a socket in a bathroom?
Socket outlets are heavily restricted by zone rules and usually impractical in domestic bathrooms. Shaver supply units are the common exception where permitted.
Why did my old bathroom lights pass years ago but fail now?
Standards and RCD expectations have tightened. An inspection applies current safety thinking to what is still in service.
bathroom-electrical-safety.seo_keywords: bathroom electrical safety, bathroom zones electrical, bathroom extractor electrician, IP rated bathroom lights, bathroom electrical safety North West

solar-inverter-installation.intro: Solar inverter installation converts DC generation from PV arrays into usable AC power and is critical to system efficiency and safety. iComply installs and electrically integrates solar inverters with correct isolation, labelling and AC connection to the property distribution system.
solar-inverter-installation.body: String sizing, ventilation clearances and shutdown devices are checked so inverters operate within manufacturer limits. We configure monitoring portals where included and verify generation readings at handover. If you already have panels but a failed inverter, we can assess like-for-like or improved replacements.
solar-inverter-installation.meta_desc: Solar inverter installation and replacement in the North West. Safe AC integration, isolation and monitoring setup.
focus_points: String and hybrid inverter electrical fit-out
faq: How long do solar inverters typically last?
Often around a decade, though environment and quality vary. Warranties differ by brand and should be registered at install.
Can I upgrade to a hybrid inverter for batteries later?
Sometimes existing PV can feed a hybrid unit; compatibility and board space need checking. We assess before you buy batteries.
Why is my inverter showing a grid fault code?
Grid voltage parameters, loose AC connections or DNO-side issues can trigger faults. Diagnosis separates inverter failure from supply conditions.
solar-inverter-installation.seo_keywords: solar inverter installation, PV inverter install, hybrid inverter installation, solar inverter replacement, solar inverter North West
